mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 50k signals weakening labor market conditions that increase recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 50k, or the signal's IC < -0.05 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO-HOUST signal shows a Rank IC > 0.10 in 3-year rolling windows, or SPX returns are positively correlated with the composite index (r > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing real interest rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPI exceeds 3.5%, or the signal's IC < -0.05 in 18-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports equity market performance over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO signal shows a Rank IC < 0.05 in 12-month rolling windows, or SPX returns are negatively correlated with INDPRO (r < -0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate (GDPC1) above 3.0% indicates strong demand conditions that boost corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's IC < 0.10 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5.5% and decline in real sales (RSAFS) below 600B signals a recessionary labor-market shock, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-RSAFS interaction shows a Rank IC > 0.10 in 24-month rolling windows, or SPX returns are positively correlated with the composite index (r > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1400k signals reduced construction activity that weakens demand for durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST falls below 1400k, or the signal's IC < -0.05 in 18-month rolling windows.
